document.name: KERNEL_SPEC
document.purpose: Define the accepted kernel baseline as the authoritative architecture for the repository. This document fixes the authority model, state flow, plane boundaries, repository semantics, invariants, artifact classes, gate model, and admission artifact convention required to move from architecture to enforcement.

interpretation: The canonical structural model is authoritative.
JSON Structure is an authoring syntax for that model, not the authority itself.
JSON Schema is derived for exported boundary contracts and interoperability.
CUE evaluates legality over normalized state.
Jsonnet renders artifacts from admitted state only.
Structurize/Avrotize is a derived interop and codegen bridge.

state_flow.pipeline_string: raw sources -> normalized state -> admitted state -> rendered artifacts
boundary_rules: Normalization may regularize, resolve, and canonicalize.
Admission determines legality, completeness, and allowed composition.
Jsonnet must never read raw authoring sources directly.

policy.description: Admission and legality over normalized state.

repo_semantics.manifests: control objects declaring bundles, projections, and generators
repo_semantics.generated: committed derived artifacts and evidence views

physical_baseline_freeze: No top-level state/ directory unless it is later reintroduced as a source declaration area.
generated/ contains committed derived artifacts and committed review/evidence views.
build/ contains ephemeral local materialization only.

normalization_contract.description: Normalization transforms raw source material into a regularized kernel dataset suitable for admission.
allowed_operations: expand declared composition into explicit normalized objects
preserve provenance links to source modules
forbidden_operations: silently discard source information unless declared
resolve semantic conflicts without emitting evidence

admission_contract.description: Admission evaluates legality over normalized state.

rules: Jsonnet renders from admitted state only.
Renderers must never read raw authoring sources directly.
Every renderer declares its admitted-state inputs and output class.

scope_and_authority.json_structure: authoring syntax only; not the authority object

plane_boundaries.manifests/: control objects declaring bundles, projections, and generators
plane_boundaries.generated/: committed derived artifacts and evidence views
plane_boundaries.downstream_compatibility_hacks: must not be elevated into the authority plane

repository_layout_obligations.forbidden_top_level_area: state/ unless later reintroduced explicitly as a source declaration area

normalization_contract.description: Normalization transforms raw source material into a regularized kernel dataset suitable for admission.
allowed_operations: expand declared composition into explicit normalized objects
preserve provenance links to source modules
forbidden_operations: silently discard source information unless declared
resolve semantic conflicts without emitting evidence

admission_contract.description: Admission evaluates legality over normalized state only.
forbidden_inputs: arbitrary mixed inputs in place of normalized state

derived_artifact_policy.exports_and_rendered_artifacts: must be treated as derived outputs
derived_artifact_policy.toolchain_versions: must be pinned or otherwise policy-controlled

manifest_obligations.requirement: Every bundle, projection, and generator must be declared by a manifest control object.
manifest_obligations.prohibition: No bundle, projection, or generator may exist as an undeclared side-effect of ad hoc tooling.

gates.name: Rendering
gates.deny_condition: renderer reads non-admitted input or render failure

gates.name: Drift and integrity
gates.deny_condition: unapproved diff, missing output, or hand-edited derived file

minimum_semantics.decision.json: allow/deny summary, policy bundle ID, input digests, and tool versions

completion_criteria: gate IDs and evidence artifacts are fixed
admission artifacts are no longer implicit

invariants: Canonical model is authoritative; exports are derived.
CUE admits normalized state, not arbitrary mixed inputs.
Jsonnet renders from admitted state only.
generated/ is committed derived state; build/ is ephemeral.
Every bundle, projection, and generator is declared by a manifest control object.
Every committed derived artifact must be regen-clean.
Normalization regularizes structure only; it does not decide legality.
Every admission run emits an explicit decision artifact.
Every renderer declares its admitted-state inputs and output class.
Toolchain versions for normalize/admit/render are pinned or policy-controlled.
No renderer, exporter, or compatibility shim may establish authority; authority exists only in the canonical structural model under structures/.

gates.name: Rendering
gates.deny_condition: renderer reads non-admitted input or render failure

gates.name: Drift and integrity
gates.deny_condition: unapproved diff, missing output, or hand-edited derived file

minimum_semantics.decision.json: allow/deny summary, policy bundle id, input digests, tool versions

completion_condition: gate IDs and evidence artifacts are fixed
admission artifacts are no longer implicit
